name: Oregon Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Oregon - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Oregon pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oregon’s pharmacy technician application totals $115: a $70 application fee plus a $45 national fingerprint background check fee. The board’s renewal FAQ lists a $104 renewal total before late fees.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Oregon have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Oregon. To work in Oregon, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Oregon?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Oregon Board of Pharmacy says pharmacy technician applications usually take about 4 to 6 weeks to process once all materials are in.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Oregon while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Oregon Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Oregon.
